title: Sieruela hanburyana
intro: Sieruela hanburyana, also known as the Desert Rose or Hanbury's Sieruela, is a succulent plant species belonging to the family Brassicaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Sieruela hanburyana include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Sieruela hanburyana is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to soothe sore throats, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Sieruela hanburyana is classified within the family Apocynaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants that includes many species of vines, shrubs, and trees. Sieruela hanburyana is further grouped into the subfamily Asclepiadoideae, which is a subfamily of Apocynaceae that primarily contains milkweed and related plants. The genus Sieruela is a relatively small group of plants within the subfamily Asclepiadoideae. Sieruela hanburyana is a species within this genus. Sieruela hanburyana is endemic to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe.
